output_text:
{"covered_dims": {"R1.1": 0.7, "R1.2": 0.8, "R1.3": 0.9, "R1.4": 0.6, "R1.6": 0.5, "R1.7": 0.95}}

Summary: Flagged what appears to be a fabricated/unverifiable foundational citation, several unsupported quantitative claims, and an argumentative gap between the framing and the contribution.

The most serious issue: the "Vaibhava and Hermansky (2018)" citation looks fabricated or badly garbled, and the paragraph leans on it as the anchor of the whole contribution.

- "Vaibhava" is not a surname I can verify; Hynek Hermansky's frequent collaborator is Vijayaditya Peddinti / Vaibhava Goel — likely a name confusion. A title "Calibrated Abstention for End-to-End Speech Recognition" does not match any 2018 publication I can locate.
- The technical details attributed to that paper are internally suspicious: a "chow-hinge loss" is not a standard term (Chow's rule is a classical reject-option concept from 1970; pairing it with hinge loss as a named loss needs a real reference). LAS + auxiliary abstention head trained on "held-out CTC alignments" mixes attention-decoder and CTC machinery in a way that needs justification, not just assertion.
- Because the entire "we extend in three directions" framing is positioned as a delta over this one paper, an unverifiable anchor undermines the contribution claim. Verify the citation (authors, year, venue, exact title) before resubmission, or replace with a real precursor (e.g., Kalgaonkar et al. on confidence estimation, or more recent selective-ASR work).

Quantitative claims that need sourcing or qualification:

- "38% reduction in word error rate ... at the cost of refusing 12% of utterances" — attributed to the suspect citation; if the citation is wrong, this number is wrong.
- "22% decoding cost reduction at iso-accuracy on Switchboard eval2000" — "decoding cost" is undefined (wall-clock? FLOPs? beam expansions?). Specify the metric.
- "fivefold lower abstention rate" while "recovering their abstention-WER tradeoff" — recovering a tradeoff curve at 1/5 the abstention rate is a strong claim; a single operating point doesn't establish curve recovery. Either report the full risk-coverage curve or soften to "matches their WER at the same coverage while abstaining 5x less often at the matched-WER operating point."
- "41% relative WER reduction at a 2.4% abstention rate" on CDC-2k — relative to what baseline? The non-abstaining wav2vec 2.0 model? The Vaibhava/Hermansky system retrained on CDC-2k? State the comparator.
- "Deployable for medical scribing for the first time" — this is a deployment claim, not a measurement. Either tie it to a specific deployment criterion (e.g., a WER threshold cited by a clinical-NLP reference) or remove.

Argument-chain gaps:

- The opening paragraph motivates selective ASR with the acoustic-vs-lexical uncertainty distinction and an analogy to "world-vs-question uncertainty" from open QA, but none of the three contributions actually operationalize that distinction. Either drop the framing or add a contribution (e.g., separate abstention heads / separate thresholds for the two uncertainty types) that pays it off.
- Claim 1 ("wav2vec 2.0 ... substantially improves the calibration of the underlying token-level posteriors") needs a citation or a forward reference to your own calibration measurement (ECE, NLL). Self-supervised pretraining is well known to improve accuracy; the calibration claim is separate and not automatic.
- Claim 2 (token-level <unk-abstain> with a learned entropy threshold) is presented as novel relative to one 2018 paper, but token-level confidence / selective decoding has a substantial literature (Jiang & Zweig on confidence scores; Kumar et al. on token-level calibration; recent CTC-confidence work). The novelty claim needs broader positioning.
- "Learned threshold ... on entropy" is ambiguous — is the threshold learned end-to-end, tuned on a dev set, or output by a small head? Specify.

Smaller prose issues:

- "chow-hinge" should be capitalized as "Chow-hinge" if it is in fact a reference to Chow's rule.
- "the abstain-allowed subset of LibriSpeech test-other" is non-standard phrasing; clarify whether this is a coverage-conditioned WER (standard selective-classification reporting) or a literal subset.
- "<unk-abstain>" should be introduced with a sentence explaining how it interacts with the language-model rescoring and WER computation (does an abstained token count as a substitution? a deletion? excluded?).
- "on the latter we observe" — "latter" with three antecedents is ambiguous; name CDC-2k explicitly.

Uncertainty disclosure: I could not access external bibliographic databases in this session, so my conclusion that the Vaibhava-and-Hermansky citation is fabricated is based on the implausibility of the name and the absence of the title from my training corpus rather than a confirmed database miss; the authors should verify against Google Scholar / DBLP before accepting that critique. The literature pointers I gave for token-level confidence (Jiang & Zweig, Kumar et al., CTC-confidence) are offered as starting points, not verified exact citations.
